title: rubus micans subsp. heterochrous
intro: Rubus micans subsp. heterochrous, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and inflammation due to its high content of anthocyanins and phenolic acids. The health benefits of thimbleberry include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Thimbleberry is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
